title: Public U-Boot A733 MMC clock/reset/transfer audit

findings.evidence: drivers/mmc/sunxi_mmc.c routes data transfers through mmc_trans_data_by_cpu(), sets SUNXI_MMC_GCTRL_ACCESS_BY_AHB, and reads/writes the FIFO directly.
findings.interpretation: U-Boot SD-card reads do not prove SMHC0 internal IDMAC descriptor fetch works.

findings.evidence: For CONFIG_MACH_SUN60I_A733, U-Boot uses MMC module clock offset 0xd00 plus 0x10 per host and divides the reported PLL6/PERIPH0 parent by 3.
findings.interpretation: This matches the known A733 SMHC0 clock-register family and does not expose a missing IDMAC-only register.

findings.evidence: U-Boot names RST_BUS_MMC0 as reset cell 19; the Linux RFC names RST_BUS_MMC0 as reset cell 36. U-Boot's clk_a733.c maps RST_BUS_MMC0 to CCU offset 0x0d0c bit 16; Linux ccu-sun60i-a733.c maps its RST_BUS_MMC0 to offset 0xd0c bit 16.
findings.interpretation: The numeric reset-cell mismatch is binding namespace drift, not a physical register delta. H075 already tested the current Linux symbolic reset and still hit the descriptor-read stall.

findings.evidence: U-Boot DTS uses compatible allwinner,sun60i-a733-mmc, allwinner,sun20i-d1-mmc with clocks ahb/mmc, reset ahb, max-frequency 150 MHz, and the same SMHC0 base 0x04020000.
findings.interpretation: No DTS property in public U-Boot explains an IDMAC-only Linux failure.

conclusion: No behavior patch. Public U-Boot confirms a PIO/AHB data path and does not exercise IDMAC. Its clock/reset mappings do not reveal a physical delta beyond already-tested reset cleanup.
next_action: Keep the active blocker focused on missing secure/fabric/handoff detail or maintainer/vendor response. Do not repeat U-Boot-derived reset-cell tests without new evidence.
